latest.title: Director/PDMR Shareholding
latest.content:
Today 15:46
RNS Number : 3647I
Tullow Oil PLC
15 June 2026
NOTIFICATION OF TRANSACTIONS OF PERSONS DISCHARGING MANAGERIAL RESPONSIBILITIES
Tullow announces that the following conditional and nil-cost option awards (each an "
Award
") over its ordinary shares of £0.10 pence each ("
Shares
") were made under the Tullow Oil plc 2023 Executive Share Plan ("
the ESP
") on 11 June 2026 to Ian Perks, Chief Executive Officer and Richard Miller, Chief Financial Officer of the Company, each being 'persons discharging managerial responsibilities'.
The vesting of the Awards depends upon the extent to which specified performance targets are met over a three-year performance period from 11 June 2026 to 10 June 2029, subject to any adjustments that are made to the vesting outcome of an Award in accordance with the rules of the ESP. Following any such vesting, the Awards will ordinarily be subject to a further two year "holding period" and the release of Shares to the relevant Award holder will be postponed until the expiry of that holding period (
i.e.
11 June 2031).
Separately, on 11 June 2026 Mr Perks and Mr Miller were each granted a cash conditional award (a "
Cash Award
") under the ESP (as amended and approved by shareholders by resolution at the annual general meeting of the Company held on 10 June 2026 (the "
2026 AGM
"). Each Cash Award represents a right for the relevant Cash Award holder to receive a cash sum in the maximum amount of 125% of their base salary (as at the date of grant of the Cash Award). The vesting of the Cash Awards (and the amount of cash payable to the relevant Cash Award holder) depends upon the extent to which specified performance targets (which relate to addressing the Company's debt maturities) are met over the vesting period of the Cash Award, subject to any adjustments that are made to the vesting outcome of an Award in accordance with the rules of the ESP. The Cash Awards have been granted in accordance with the terms of the new Directors' Remuneration Policy which was approved by resolution of the shareholders at the 2026 AGM. For the avoidance of doubt, the Cash Awards do not confer a right to receive shares, and the cash value that is receivable is not related to the price of a share - accordingly, details of the Cash Awards are not included in the regulatory tables set out in the remainder of this announcement.

latest.title: AGM Trading Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 7308H
Tullow Oil PLC
10 June 2026
Tullow Oil plc
AGM Trading Update
10 June 2026
-
Tullow Oil plc (Tullow) issues the following statement in advance of the Group's Annual General Meeting (AGM). The meeting will take place at Tullow Oil plc, Building 9, Chiswick Park, London, W4 5XT at 11:00 BST today. We are also enabling shareholders to listen to the AGM as well as participate remotely. Instructions for joining the audio cast can be found at the end of this statement or within the
Notice of Meeting
.
The information contained herein has not been audited and may be subject to further review and amendment.
Ian Perks, Chief Executive Officer of Tullow, commented:
"Tullow has performed strongly since the outset of 2026, marked by significant delivery across the business. Our drilling campaign in Ghana continues to progress well with encouraging results to date reinforcing our growing confidence in delivering production at the higher end of our guidance range, reflecting our disciplined execution and focus on high operational performance.
"With group production at the higher end of guidance and our significant leverage to the oil price, including year to date realisations ahead of expectations, we remain well placed to generate significant free cash flow in 2026 and beyond. Supported by a stable platform and strong momentum, we remain focused on executing our growth strategy to realise the full potential of our assets in Ghana and deliver value for all stakeholders."
Operational update
·
Group working interest production from January to May 2026 was 43.1 kboepd, including 7.3 kboepd of gas production, supporting the expectation of being at the higher end of the previously announced 2026 range of 34-42 kboepd.
·
The drill schedule for 2026 remains unchanged, with the third of six Jubilee wells (J76-P) expected onstream this week. Logging results of the well are supportive of a strong production well.
·
The remaining two producer wells (J77-P and J50-P) are expected onstream in June and July, with the final well (water injection) due onstream in September.
·
The outstanding operational uptime performance in the first quarter of 2026 continued in April and May, with facility uptime across both Jubilee and TEN FPSOs averaging more than 99% for January to May.
·
Greater Jubilee Plan of Further Development has been approved by the Minister of Energy, which confirms support for further drilling of up to 20 wells in Jubilee after the end of the current campaign.
Financial update
·
2026 free cash flow guidance remains $70-175 million at $70-100/bbl. This range is expected to grow to $110-230 million if an additional cargo can be delivered in December 2026.
·
Average pre-hedge oil price realisations for five cargos, from January to May, of c.$96/bbl (c.$87/bbl post-hedge), with Tullow's May Jubilee cargo achieving $119/bbl.
·
Commodity hedge portfolio protecting 60% downside whilst retaining access to 60% upside in 2026.
·
Group capital expenditure and decommissioning spend guidance for 2026 remains unchanged at c.$200 million and c.$25 million.
AGM audio cast
11:00am BST
We are enabling shareholders to listen to a live audio cast of the AGM, as well as participate remotely.
In order to participate at the meeting, you will need to visit www.meetnow.global/TULLAGM2026 on your device operating a compatible browser using the latest version of Chrome, Firefox, Edge or Safari. Please note that Internet Explorer is not supported. It is highly recommended that you check your system capabilities in advance of the meeting day.
If you are a shareholder, you can use your unique Shareholder Reference Number and PIN as displayed on your Form of Proxy/Attendance Card. If you are an appointed proxy or a corporate representative, you will have had to be provided with a unique invite code to enter the meeting and exercise your rights. These credentials will be issued one working day prior to the meeting, conditional on evidence of your proxy appointment or corporate representative appointment having been received and accepted. If you have not been provided with your meeting access credentials, please ensure you contact Computershare on the morning of the meeting, but no later than one hour before the start of the meeting.
Access to the meeting via www.meetnow.global/TULLAGM2026 will be available from 10.00am. During the meeting, you must ensure you are connected to the internet at all times in order to vote when the Chair commences polling on Resolutions being put to the meeting. Therefore, it is your responsibility to ensure connectivity for the duration of the meeting.
Technical issues
If you experience any technical issues with the site, you may either call our Registrar on the telephone number provided on the site or once you have entered the meeting, you can raise your question using the chat function. If you have technical issues prior to the start of the meeting, you should contact our Registrar on the shareholder helpline.
Process
The process of asking questions and accessing the AGM audio casting will be further explained within the application and located on the information page.
CONTACTS
Tullow Investor Relations
ir@tullowoil.com
Matthew Evans
Mel Folahan (Deputy Company Secretary)
Camarco (Media)
(+44 20 3757 4980)
Billy Clegg
Georgia Edmonds
Rebecca Waterworth
Notes to editors
Tullow is an independent energy company committed to building a better future through the responsible oil and gas development of its core producing assets in Ghana. The Group is quoted on the London and Ghanaian stock exchanges (symbol: TLW). For further information, please refer to:
www.tullowoil.com
.
Follow Tullow on:
LinkedIn:
www.linkedin.com/company/Tullow-Oil
X:
www.X.com/TullowOilplc

company_30s.what_they_do: TLW.L is a publicly listed company that appears to focus on asset management and capital restructuring, with recent activity around selling Kenyan assets and securing refinancing. It operates in a sector that relies on large-scale infrastructure or resource projects.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company’s ability to raise capital and offload non‑core assets positions it to streamline operations and potentially invest in higher‑growth opportunities. In a market where capital efficiency drives valuation, a successful restructuring can unlock shareholder value.
company_30s.current_state: TLW.L has recently completed a refinancing transaction and sold a significant portion of its Kenyan assets. The board has appointed new independent directors, and the company is preparing for its annual AGM. The share price is trading near its recent valuation, but there is no visible upside or downside trend in the current snapshot.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy updating its governance structure—new director shareholdings, voting rights, and independent directors—signalling a drive for stronger oversight.
A major refinancing deal was closed and an asset sale in Kenya was reported, indicating a shift toward a leaner balance sheet.
Regulatory filings, including the annual report, AGM notice, and consent solicitation, suggest the firm is preparing for a strategic decision that could reshape its capital base.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet watchfulness – traders are waiting for a clear move before committing.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few voices point to the refinancing and asset sale as a green light for a bounce; they believe the company is shedding debt and focusing on profitable assets.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others caution that a block listing application and the need for shareholder consent could stall progress, raising fears of a prolonged downturn or even delisting.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has moved from vague speculation to a more focused debate about the company’s restructuring plan. Optimism is present but tempered by concerns over regulatory hurdles.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for TLW.L are flat, giving the setup maximum surprise potential if a catalyst materialises.

near_term_catalysts: Confirmation that the refinancing has secured the needed liquidity and that the new capital structure is in place.
Official announcement of the Kenyan asset sale closing, including the proceeds and how they will be used.
Any new project or partnership announcement that could replace the sold assets and provide a clear growth path.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike that breaks the current low‑volume plateau, a price break above the 20‑day moving average, or a regulatory approval that clears the block listing application.
expect_next.timing_regime: The company is likely in the early stages of a recovery cycle—capital restructuring is underway, but the market has not yet priced in the potential upside. A strong APEX score indicates tight compression, so a breakout would be a clear sign of mean reversion.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the refinancing and asset sale close successfully, the company could reduce debt by a substantial amount and free up cash for new projects. A 30‑50% upside on the current trading price would be achievable if the market reacts positively to a clearer capital plan.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The block listing application could delay or derail the restructuring, and regulatory approvals might be delayed. A worst‑case scenario is a prolonged flat or further decline if the market doubts the company’s ability to execute the plan.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 30–50%. Potential downside: up to the current trading price if the company fails to secure the necessary approvals. The asymmetry is moderate, favouring a cautious long stance rather than a hard bet.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a breakout above the 20‑day moving average with a stop just below the recent low. Allocate 10–15% of the trading account, keeping the rest in reserve for a follow‑through if the price continues upward.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until you see a volume surge of at least 50% over the 5‑day average and a clear announcement confirming the refinancing or asset sale. Only then consider a small position to test the waters.
playbook.if_passing: If the company is delisted or files for bankruptcy, exit immediately. No other terminal risks have been identified, so a passing scenario is unlikely.

ai_insights.bottom_line: TLW.L is in a restructuring phase with no clear contrarian signal yet; watch for the refinancing and Kenyan asset sale to become the catalyst that could trigger a meaningful bounce.
